c.d: The size of the app's active user community and market adoption level which impacts collaboration effectiveness.
Number and quality of third-party app integrations (e.g., Gmail, Google Drive, Zoom) facilitating seamless workflow.
Includes real-time messaging, video/audio calls, file sharing, channels, and notifications that enable team interaction.
Capabilities such as task assignment, status tracking, checklists, boards, and reporting to organize collaborative work.
Availability of features for visual teamwork like mind maps, diagrams, templates, and virtual whiteboards.
Measures and features ensuring data protection, encryption, and compliance for sensitive collaborations.
Ability to support teams from small groups to large enterprises with flexible user limits and feature upgrades.

c.n: Ease of use and user interface
c.d: How intuitive and accessible the app is for different users, promoting efficient collaboration.
